The API confirms the approach. Program 3's structure (Richards + affine seed shift) scored 0.9161, the highest in context. I'll adopt that proven-better structure for the parent.

The reasoning: the parent ignores x2 (seed concentration) entirely and uses a symmetric logistic. ThT amyloid growth curves are asymmetric, and seeding advances the curve midpoint. Adding (a) an affine seed shift to the half-time and (b) a Richards shape exponent both proved to improve validation NMSE. Both extensions are numerically safe: the affine term avoids singularities (unlike a power of x2 which is often 0), and the Richards base (1+exp(z)) ≥ 1 so any real power is finite.
